name: Governance, Access & Provenance
purpose: Let users understand whether and how the dataset can be accessed and reused (policy + operational access steps), without needing back-and-forth.
effort: Low - Medium (often shareable, but sometimes requires coordination with governance teams. Often easy if links exist; harder if docs are scattered; generally safe to share)

fields.name: studyID
fields.description: The stable, unique identifier for a study that never changes, even if other study metadata changes (like the study name, short name, owner, or project). Example: STUDY_SCA

fields.name: optional persistent row identifier
fields.description: Use studyID as the anchor, plus a persistent row identifier to identify a specific file/version, etc.

fields.name: optional persistent row identifier  - value
fields.description: Enter a unique identifier for the selection made.

fields.name: appliesTo
fields.description: Indicates the level of entity to which a metadata value applies (e.g., individual participant or population summary).

valid_values.definition: The metadata value applies to an individual participant entity (participant-level).
The metadata value applies to a cohort or group of participants considered as a unit.

fields.name: aggregationLevel
fields.description: Specifies whether a metadata value represents individual-level data or an aggregate summary

valid_values.definition: The metadata value represents an individual-level value (not aggregated).
The metadata value represents an aggregate summary across a defined population scope.

fields.name: populationScope
fields.description: Identifies the population described by the metadata (e.g., study-wide, cohort, or study arm).

valid_values.definition: The metadata value summarizes or pertains to the full study population.
The metadata value summarizes or pertains to a specific study arm or group.
The metadata value summarizes or pertains to a defined cohort within the study.

fields.name: externallyRequestable
fields.description: Availability indicator to request the data.

valid_values.definition: The data are available for external request.
The data are not available for external request.
Only a subset of the data is available for external request.
External requestability is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: irbApprovalNumber
fields.description: The identifier assigned by the IRB for an approved or reviewed study, if applicable.

fields.name: irbStatus
fields.description: The regulatory determination of IRB oversight for the study.

valid_values.definition: Study reviewed and approved by an IRB
Study determined to be exempt from IRB review
IRB review or consent requirements waived
IRB review not required under applicable regulations
IRB determination not yet finalized
IRB status not known or not recorded

fields.name: irbInstitution
fields.description: The institution or ethics committee responsible for IRB review or determination.

fields.name: irbExemptionType
fields.description: The regulatory exemption category under which the study was determined to be exempt from IRB review.

valid_values.definition: Educational practices
Educational tests, surveys, interviews, public behavior
Benign behavioral interventions
Secondary research using identifiable data or biospecimens
Public benefit or service programs
Taste and food quality studies
Exempt under non–Common Rule framework
Exemption type not known or not recorded

fields.name: irbApprovalDescription
fields.description: Free-text description providing additional context about IRB approval, exemption, or oversight.

fields.name: irbScope
fields.description: Scope of IRB in relation to the study.

valid_values.definition: IRB oversight pertains to the original conduct of the study (initial enrollment and primary data collection).
IRB oversight pertains to secondary analysis of previously collected data (e.g., reuse of an existing dataset).
IRB oversight pertains to a specific site within a multi-site study (e.g., local IRB approvals).
IRB oversight pertains to sharing or reuse of data outside the original study context (e.g., repository deposition or downstream reuse).

fields.name: consentType
fields.description: Consent type (broad, tiered, etc.)

valid_values.definition: Consent permits future research use without restriction to specific diseases or narrowly defined purposes.
Consent permits some types of research uses while restricting others (i.e., multiple allowed-use options are specified).
Consent limits data use to a specific disease, condition, purpose, or study context.
Consent does not permit sharing beyond the original study team or stated original purpose.
Consent type is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: deidentificationMethod
fields.description: De-ID method applied

valid_values.definition: Direct identifiers were removed according to HIPAA Safe Harbor criteria.
An expert assessed and documented that re-identification risk is sufficiently low under applicable standards.
Direct identifiers were replaced with codes while a re-identification key may exist under separate control.
Identifiers were removed or transformed such that re-identification is not reasonably possible.
Data were shared as a HIPAA limited dataset (some indirect identifiers such as dates may be retained under agreement).
De-identification method is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: accessRestrictions
fields.description: Access level (ex., open, controlled, embargoed)

fields.name: dataUseConditions
fields.description: Data use conditions

fields.name: accessProcedure
fields.description: Brief description of the steps required to request and obtain access to the dataset.
fields.example: Submit data access request and institutional approval

fields.name: accessRequestURL
fields.description: URL to the access request page/portal or repository instructions for obtaining access.

fields.name: dataLocation
fields.description: Where the dataset is hosted or distributed (e.g., Synapse project, NIMH/NDAR collection, dbGaP).

valid_values.definition: Dataset is hosted and distributed via a Synapse project or file entity.
Dataset is hosted within the NIMH Data Archive (formerly NDAR) as a collection or study.
Dataset is deposited in the NIH Database of Genotypes and Phenotypes (dbGaP).
Dataset is hosted on Zenodo, typically with a DOI and public or restricted access settings.
Dataset is hosted on the Open Science Framework (OSF) platform.
Dataset is hosted in an institution-managed repository (e.g., university or hospital repository).
Dataset is hosted on a platform not listed above.
Hosting/distribution location is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: accessContact
fields.description: Point of contact (team, email alias, or role) for access questions.

fields.name: accessReviewBody
fields.description: The entity that reviews/approves access requests (e.g., DAC, IRB, sponsor).

valid_values.definition: Access requests are reviewed and approved by a designated Data Access Committee.
Access requests require review or approval by an IRB or ethics committee.
Access requests are reviewed and approved through repository governance processes (not a separate DAC).
Access requests require approval by the study’s principal investigator(s).
Access requests are reviewed by a body not listed above.
The approving/reviewing body is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: accessRequirements
fields.description: Checklist of materials required for access (e.g., DUA, IRB letter, training).

valid_values.definition: A data use agreement must be executed before access is granted.
Proof of IRB/ethics approval is required prior to access.
An IRB/ethics exemption determination is acceptable in lieu of full approval.
A documented plan for secure data handling/storage is required.
Completion of human subjects research training is required for requestors.
A specific account or repository identifier is required (e.g., Synapse account, eRA Commons).
Additional requirements apply that are not listed above.
Access requirements are not known or not recorded.
